Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given a list of 18 events from multiple cases There is no explicit case ID so we must group events by patient journey

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: We can look at attributes that are consistent for a patient patient_age and patient_gender However note that two patients might have the same age and gender so we must also consider the sequence of activities and timing

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Lets list the events with some key attributes

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Initial triage age 45 M department ER time 081050 likely same as event 1 45M ER

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Initial triage age 62 F department ER time 081229 likely same as event 2 62F ER

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Doctor consultation age 28 M department Surgery time 081300 likely same as event 3 28M Surgery

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now event 7 Xray imaging age 45 M department Radiology time 081542 could be the 45M from ER event 1 and 4 because after triage in ER they might go to Radiology

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Event 8 Lab test requested age 62 F department ER time 081610 likely the 62F event 2 and 5 in ER

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Event 9 Surgery preparation age 28 M department Surgery time 081645 likely the 28M event 3 and 6 in Surgery

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Event 10 Lab test completed age 62 F department Laboratory time 082027 likely the 62F event 8 was lab test requested now completed

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Event 11 Doctor consultation age 62 F department ER time 082254 likely the 62F now in ER again

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Event 12 Doctor consultation age 45 M department ER time 082310 likely the 45M who had an Xray returning to ER for consultation

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Event 13 Surgery performed age 28 M department Surgery time 082519 likely the 28M after preparation

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Event 14 Prescription created age 62 F department ER time 082700 likely the 62F

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Event 15 Patient discharged age 45 M department ER time 083005 likely the 45M

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Event 16 Recovery room age 28 M department Surgery time 083137 likely the 28M after surgery

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Event 17 Patient discharged age 62 F department ER time 083529 likely the 62F

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Event 18 Patient discharged age 28 M department Surgery time 084013 likely the 28M after recovery

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now lets group by patient Patient A 45 year old male started in ER Events 1 4 7 12 15 Patient B 62 year old female started in ER Events 2 5 8 10 11 14 17 Patient C 28 year old male started in Surgery Events 3 6 9 13 16 18

Name: Validation - PE
Text: We can check the order and departments

Name: Validation - PE
Text: For Patient A 45M Register at ER event 1 Initial triage in ER event 4 Xray in Radiology event 7 note department changed to Radiology then back to ER for consultation event 12 Doctor consultation in ER event 12 Discharged from ER event 15

Name: Validation - PE
Text: For Patient B 62F Register at ER event 2 Initial triage in ER event 5 Lab test requested in ER event 8 Lab test completed in Laboratory event 10 department changed to Laboratory then back to ER for consultation event 11 Doctor consultation in ER event 11 Prescription created in ER event 14 Discharged from ER event 17

Name: Validation - PE
Text: For Patient C 28M Register at Surgery event 3 Doctor consultation in Surgery event 6 Surgery preparation in Surgery event 9 Surgery performed in Surgery event 13 Recovery room in Surgery event 16 Discharged from Surgery event 18

Name: Validation - PE
Text: This grouping makes sense because each patients events are in a logical order and the departments and activities are consistent

Name: Validation - PE
Text: However note that event 12 for Patient A 45M is a doctor consultation in ER which comes after the Xray Also note that event 12 is at 082310 which is after event 11 082254 and event 10 082027 The timeline for Patient A event 1 080513 event 4 081050 event 7 081542 event 12 082310 event 15 083005 That seems reasonable

Name: Validation - PE
Text: For Patient B event 2 080702 event 5 081229 event 8 081610 event 10 082027 event 11 082254 event 14 082700 event 17 083529 This also seems reasonable

Name: Validation - PE
Text: For Patient C event 3 080715 event 6 081300 event 9 081645 event 13 082519 event 16 083137 event 18 084013 Note that the surgery preparation 081645 is after the consultation 081300 and before the surgery 082519 The recovery 083137 and discharge 084013 follow

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Therefore we have three cases

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Lets assign case IDs we can call them Case 1 Case 2 Case 3 but note that the original data didnt have them We can just output the grouping

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Since the task is to infer the case groupings we can list the events for each case

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: However note that the problem does not require us to output the events again but to determine the grouping We can present the grouping by listing the event numbers for each case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1 Patient 45M ER journey Events 1 4 7 12 15 Case 2 Patient 62F ER journey Events 2 5 8 10 11 14 17 Case 3 Patient 28M Surgery journey Events 3 6 9 13 16 18

Name: Validation - PE
Text: We can check if there are any other attributes that might conflict but this seems clear

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the answer is that there are three cases and we can list the event numbers for each case
